message.content:
You are working in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service (NestJS/TypeScript monorepo).

## Your Task
Fix ESLint `custom-rules/require-object-params` violations for Jira ticket RECO-688.
Ticket URL: https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-688

## Context
- Current branch: `RECO-686-fix-request-store-util`
- You must create a NEW branch from this branch (NOT from main)
- Branch name format: `RECO-688-<kebab-summary>`
- Commit format: `RECO-688: <imperative description>`

## ESLint Rule
`custom-rules/require-object-params` — all exported functions must use a single object destructuring parameter instead of positional params.

**Pattern:**
```typescript
// ❌ Before
export function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}
// ✅ After
export function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```

## Steps

### 1. Find the target file
Run ESLint to find remaining files with violations:
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 src/ --format=compact 2>&1 | grep "require-object-params" | head -40
```
Pick an appropriate unfixed file for RECO-688.

### 2. Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b RECO-688-<descriptive-name>
```
Branch from current branch `RECO-686-fix-request-store-util`.

### 3. Fix violations in target file(s)
- Change positional params to `{ param }: { param: type }` destructuring
- Update ALL internal calls to changed functions
- Find ALL callers: use Grep tool to search for function names across src/ and test/
- Update every call site
- Do NOT use `{ ...param }` spread shortcuts

### 4. Update tests
Find and update all test files that call changed functions.

### 5. Run tests
```bash
npm run test:unit -- --testPathPatterns="<pattern>" 2>&1
```

### 6. Run type-check
```bash
npm run type-check 2>&1
```

### 7. Verify ESLint
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 <changed-files> 2>&1
```

### 8. Commit (only relevant files — NOT .husky/pre-push)
```bash
git add <specific-files-only>
git commit -m "RECO-688: <description>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

### 9. Push
```bash
git push -u origin RECO-688-<branch-name>
```

### 10. Create DRAFT PR
Base = `RECO-686-fix-request-store-util` (NOT main).

```bash
gh pr create --draft --base RECO-686-fix-request-store-util --title "RECO-688: ..." --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
### Ticket 🐗

JIRA task: [RECO-688](https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-688)
Design: N/A

### Changes 🍍

<describe changes>

### PR Checks 🐊

- [x] I've self-reviewed my code.
- [x] I've added the necessary labels.
- [x] I've added tests for my code.
- [ ] I've updated the documentation.

### Description / Ticket Summary 🐙

Fix ESLint custom-rules/require-object-params violations — refactored functions to use single object destructuring parameter pattern.

### Screenshots / Short Videos 🐮

N/A
EOF
)"
```

## Critical Rules
- NEVER modify `.husky/pre-push`
- NEVER use relative imports (use `@utils/`, `@modules/`, etc.)
- NO comments in code
- All functions need explicit return types
- Do NOT use `{ ...spread }` as shortcut

## Return
1. The new branch name
2. The PR URL
3. Summary of files changed

content.text:
Now I can see the violations. Let me look at the specific lines:

- `style-alternative.model.ts` line 130: `upsertStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)`
- `style-alternatives.service.ts` line 20: `storeStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)`
- `styles-mv.model.ts` lines 205, 218, 232, 242: private methods

Let me look at those specific lines:

The private methods in `styles-mv.model.ts` have single non-object params. These are private methods, so the rule applies to exported functions. Wait - let me re-check what the rule actually targets. The violations are at lines 205, 218, 232, 242 which are `parseStylesMvData`, `parseJsonField`, `sanitizeBrandNames`, and `formatValidationErrors`. These are private methods. Let me check the ESLint rule to understand what it targets:

content.text:
The rule applies to ALL methods, including private ones. So I need to update private methods too when they have single non-object params. Now let me understand all the violations:

1. `style-alternative.model.ts` line 130: `upsertStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)` - single non-object param
2. `style-alternatives.service.ts` line 20: `storeStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)` - single non-object param
3. `styles-mv.model.ts` line 205: `parseStylesMvData(data: IStylesMvBase)` - private, single param
4. `styles-mv.model.ts` line 218: `parseJsonField<T>(field: T | string | null)` - private, single param
5. `styles-mv.model.ts` line 232: `sanitizeBrandNames(brands: string[])` - private, single param
6. `styles-mv.model.ts` line 242: `formatValidationErrors(errors: ValidationError[])` - private, single param

Now let me find all callers before creating the branch:

content.text: Good. The private methods are only called within the model itself. Now let me look at the e2e test and unit tests:

input.description: Check how these functions are called in e2e tests

content.text:
Good. The e2e tests are calling `storeStyleAlternative({ ... })` and `upsertStyleAlternative({ ... })` already using object syntax — that's fine.

Now let me look at what the `storeStyleAlternative` call looks like in e2e and what changes are needed:

input.description: Check e2e test calls to storeStyleAlternative

content.text:
The e2e tests already call with object syntax. But `storeStyleAlternative` needs to change its signature from `data: IStyleAlternativeCreate` to `{ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate }`, and `upsertStyleAlternative` similarly.

Wait, let me re-read the rule: these functions must use **single object destructuring parameter**. So if a function takes `data: IStyleAlternativeCreate`, that needs to become `{ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate }`. 

But looking at the e2e tests - they call `service.storeStyleAlternative({ ... })` where the `{ ... }` IS the `data` object (IStyleAlternativeCreate). So the pattern in the test is actually passing a plain object that IS `data`. After refactoring the param from `data` to `{ data }`, calls like `storeStyleAlternative({ style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... })` would break.

Let me look more carefully at how the e2e test calls it:

input.description: Look at storeStyleAlternative calls in e2e test

content.text:
I see! The e2e tests call `storeStyleAlternative({ style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... })` — passing an `IStyleAlternativeCreate` directly. After the change, the function signature becomes `storeStyleAlternative({ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate })`, so calls need to be `storeStyleAlternative({ data: { style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... } })`.

Wait — but looking at other already-fixed files in the codebase, maybe the pattern the team is using is different. Let me look at other fixed services for guidance:

input.description: Check a fixed service for the pattern used
Check the style-color model for upsertStyle signature

content.content: 222:	async upsertStyle({

input.description: Check upsertStyle signature in style-color model

content.text:
I see the pattern: `data` is wrapped inside `{ data }`. So `upsertStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)` should become `upsertStyleAlternative({ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate })` and call sites should pass `{ data: ... }` instead of just `data`.

Now let me check how the model's `upsertStyleAlternative` is called in the service and test:
- In service: `this.styleAlternativeModel.upsertStyleAlternative(data)` → needs to become `this.styleAlternativeModel.upsertStyleAlternative({ data })`
- In e2e test: `model.upsertStyleAlternative({ style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... })` → needs to become `model.upsertStyleAlternative({ data: { style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... } })`

For `storeStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)`:
- After change: `storeStyleAlternative({ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate })`
- Internal call: `this.styleAlternativeModel.upsertStyleAlternative({ data })` - already fixed
- e2e test calls: `service.storeStyleAlternative({ style_id: ..., ... })` → `service.storeStyleAlternative({ data: { style_id: ..., ... } })`

Good. The unit tests for `styles-mv.model` don't directly call the private methods (`parseStylesMvData`, `parseJsonField`, `sanitizeBrandNames`, `formatValidationErrors`), so no test changes needed for those. The unit tests only call the public methods via the model.

Now let me look at the e2e test for style-alternatives to understand which calls need updating:

Now I understand the full picture. The e2e tests call:
- `model.upsertStyleAlternative({ style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... })` — this is passing `IStyleAlternativeCreate` directly as the argument
- `service.storeStyleAlternative({ style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... })` — same pattern

After the fix:
- `upsertStyleAlternative` must take `{ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate }` 
- `storeStyleAlternative` must take `{ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate }`

So the e2e test calls need to wrap in `{ data: ... }`:
- `model.upsertStyleAlternative({ data: { style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... } })`
- `service.storeStyleAlternative({ data: { style_id: ..., alternative_style_id: ... } })`

content.content: https://github.com/customink/recommendations-service/pull/494

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary:

**Branch**: `RECO-688-fix-style-alternatives-styles-mv-require-object-params`

**PR URL**: https://github.com/customink/recommendations-service/pull/494

**Files Changed**:

1. `/src/modules/db/style-alternatives/models/style-alternative.model.ts` — Changed `upsertStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)` to `upsertStyleAlternative({ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate })`

2. `/src/modules/db/style-alternatives/style-alternatives.service.ts` — Changed `storeStyleAlternative(data: IStyleAlternativeCreate)` to `storeStyleAlternative({ data }: { data: IStyleAlternativeCreate })`, updated internal call and `LogExecution` param formatter

3. `/src/modules/db/styles-mv/models/styles-mv.model.ts` — Changed 4 private methods (`parseStylesMvData`, `parseJsonField`, `sanitizeBrandNames`, `formatValidationErrors`) to use object destructuring; updated all 8 internal call sites

4. `/test/db/style-alternatives.e2e-spec.ts` — Updated 7 call sites for `storeStyleAlternative` and `upsertStyleAlternative` to pass `{ data: ... }` instead of raw objects
